title: Use Aveva AGV Command Functions For Remove Recover Pause All And Resume All
summary: This source-specific runbook captures the training-video-supported process for accessing the Aveva-exposed AGV command set and selecting one of the documented AGV control functions: remove AGV, recover AGV, pause all AGVs, or resume all AGVs. The source confirms these commands are available in Aveva as selected Geek+ RMS features, but does not provide detailed execution guardrails, confirmations, or command-specific input rules beyond what is visible on the referenced training material.
when_to_use: Use when L2 support needs to access the Aveva interface/API area that exposes selected Geek+ RMS AGV commands and invoke one of the documented actions shown in the source: remove AGV, recover AGV, pause all AGVs, or resume all AGVs.
not_for: Do not use when the intended command behavior, required inputs, or safe-use conditions are unclear.
Do not use as a detailed per-command operating procedure; this source does not provide command-specific confirmations, prerequisites, or safety constraints.
Do not enter AGV-specific identifiers or other inputs unless those identifiers or inputs are explicitly supported by the interface shown in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the Aveva AGV command area, identify the documented command options, select the needed command, and execute only within the limits supported by the source and interface.

safety_notes: These commands can affect AGV fleet behavior.
The source does not provide detailed per-command safety constraints or guardrails.
Escalate if intended command behavior, required inputs, or safe-use conditions are unclear.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Aveva interface or API page with AGV commands

access_required: Access to the Aveva interface or API page with AGV commands
system_preconditions: The Aveva interface/API area exposing the AGV command functions must be available.

success_criteria: The user can access the Aveva-exposed AGV command set.
The documented command options can be identified: remove AGV, recover AGV, pause all AGVs, and resume all AGVs.
One documented AGV control action is selected and invoked using only source-supported interface information.
healthy_conditions: The Aveva AGV command area is accessible.
The command list matches the source-supported commands.
Any required inputs are clearly supported by the visible interface.
The selected command can be executed without relying on undocumented assumptions.
failure_conditions: The Aveva AGV command area cannot be located or accessed.
The documented command names are not visible or do not match the source.
Required command inputs, confirmations, or safe-use conditions are unclear.
Execution appears to require undocumented parameters or behavior not supported by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the intended command behavior is unclear.
